segments.text: Can you pull out Leviathan with a hook? Can you tie its mouth shut?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Can you thread a rope through its nose? Can you pass a hook through its jaw?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Will it beg you to let it go? Or will it talk softly to you?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Will it make a contract with you? Will it agree to be your slave forever?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Will you play with it like a pet bird? Will you put it on a leash for your girls?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Will your trading partners decide on a price for him, and divide him up among the merchants?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Can you pierce his skin with many harpoons, its head with fishing spears?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If you were to grab hold of it, imagine the battle you would have! You wouldn't do that again!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Any hope to capture it is foolish. Anyone who tries is thrown to the ground.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Since no one has the courage to provoke Leviathan, who would dare to st and up against me?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who has confronted me with any claim that I should repay? Everything under heaven belongs to me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Let me tell you about Leviathan: its powerful legs and graceful proportions.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who can remove its hide? Who can penetrate its double coat of armor?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who can open its jaws? Its teeth are terrifying!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its pride is its rows of scales, closed tightly together.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its scales are so close together that no air can pass between them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Each scale attaches to the next; they lock together and nothing can penetrate them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When it sneezes light shines out. Its eyes are like the rising sun.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Flames pour from its mouth, sparks of fire shoot out.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Smoke comes from its nostrils, like steam from a kettle on a fire made of reeds.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its breath sets fire to charcoal as flames shoot from its mouth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its neck is powerful, and all who face him shake with terror.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its body is dense and solid, as if it is made from cast metal.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its heart is rock-hard, like a millstone.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When it rises, even the powerful are terrified; they retreat as it thrashes about.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Swords just bounce off it, as do spears, darts, and javelins.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It brushes aside iron like straw, and bronze like rotten wood.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Arrows cannot make it run away; stones from slingshots are like pieces of stubble.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Clubs are also treated like stubble; it laughs at the sound made by flying spears.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Its underparts are covered with points as sharp as broken pots; when it drags itself through the mud it leaves marks like a threshing sledge.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It churns up the sea like water in a boiling pot, like a steaming bowl when ointment is mixed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It leaves a glistening wake behind it as if the sea had white hair.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There is nothing on earth like it: a creature that has no fear.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It looks down on all other creatures. It is the proudest of all.
segments.speaker: God
